old_value: {"id":"TGR-l8h","title":"Fix √g coefficient bug in perturbation crosscheck","description":"In examples/15_perturbation_spectrum_crosscheck.jl line 49, the coefficient 1//8 in the (tr h)² term of sqrt_g_correction() should be 1//4. This is derived from δ²(√g·L)/√g₀ = δ²L + (1/2)tr(h)·δL + L₀·[−(1/2)h^{ab}h_{ab} + (1/4)(tr h)²]. The wrong coefficient distorts the kinetic structure, pulling weight from spin-2 into gauge sectors, causing non-zero spin-1 and spin-0w. Fix the coefficient, then validate FP sanity check at Λ→0: Tr(K·P²)≈2.5k², spin-1=0, spin-0w=0.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-12T18:52:04Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-12T18:52:04Z"}

Backward-compatible: all existing tests must pass unchanged. ~150 lines changed.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-12T18:54:06Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-12T18:54:06Z"}

new_value: {"notes":"Session 27 findings: pre-built FP kernel passes all checks perfectly. Bug is in perturbation engine → Fourier → simplify path: canonicalizer fails to combine equivalent tensor structures with mixed index positions (e.g. h[d1,-d1] vs h[-d1,d1] are both tr(h) but not recognized as same). This produces 8 Fourier terms instead of 4 for flat-space δ²R, yielding spin-2 off by 2.5x. Also: TGR-l8h √g coeff fix (1/8→1/4) appears wrong — 1/8 is correct for Taylor-coefficient convention. Root cause: canonicalize + collect_terms in simplify pipeline doesn't normalize trace expressions with symmetric tensors at mixed index positions in momentum space.","status":"open"}

old_value: {"id":"TGR-dp3","title":"Canonicalizer fails to combine symmetric tensor traces at mixed index positions","description":"In Fourier-transformed perturbation expressions, h[d1,-d1] and h[-d1,d1] (both tr(h)) are not recognized as identical by canonicalize+collect_terms. This causes δ²R to produce 8 Fourier terms instead of 4 (FP kernel), with spin-2 projection off by factor 5/2. Reproducer: compute δricci_scalar(mp,2) on flat background, Fourier transform, spin_project — gives 10.625 instead of 4.25 for spin-2. Pre-built build_FP_momentum_kernel works perfectly, confirming projectors are correct. Blocker for TGR-76k.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-13T15:48:55Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-13T15:48:55Z"}

{"description":"CORRECTED DIAGNOSIS (session 28): The crosscheck failure is NOT in collect_terms/canonicalize. spin_project correctly handles shared dummies and mixed index positions (verified with FP kernel). The real bug: δ²R from the perturbation engine has A-type coefficient 5/4 (h^{ab}h_{ab}k²) instead of expected 1/2, giving spin-2=6.25 instead of 2.5. Likely a convention mismatch between expand_perturbation and the crosscheck formula, or a bug in the second-order inverse metric expansion. See HANDOFF-next-session.md for deep research plan.","title":"δ²R perturbation engine gives wrong spin-2 coefficient (misdiagnosed as canonicalizer bug)"}

new_value: {"notes":"Session 30: Root cause fully identified. Two interacting bugs: (1) to_fourier mishandles ∂(product) for quadratic forms — treats as k×product instead of distributing via Leibniz with correct momentum routing; (2) contract_metrics can't contract metrics with TDeriv partners. δ²Ric has 2 unexpanded ∂(h×∂h) terms giving spurious spin-2=2.5 and 18 terms with uncontracted g's giving 1.25. Total 3.75 vs correct 0. Simplest fix: Option D — detect and drop total-divergence terms before Fourier transform. See HANDOFF-next-session.md for full analysis."}

old_value: {"id":"TGR-7fj","title":"Document and test -2 sign convention for R²/Ric² kernel contributions","description":"The full kinetic kernel for S = ∫√g(κR + α₁R² + α₂Ric²) on flat background is K = κ·K_EH - 2α₁·K_{R²} - 2α₂·K_{Ric²}. The -2 factor was verified against Buoninfante form factors (2012.11829 Eq.2.13). The perturbation engine pipeline uses: K_EH = extract_kernel_v2(h^{ab}δ¹G_{ab}), K_R² = extract_kernel_v2((δ¹R)²), K_Ric² = extract_kernel_v2((δ¹Ric)²). The -2 sign is NOT a bug in the extraction; it comes from the relationship between the action and the kinetic operator. Must add tests verifying all 4 combinations (κ only, +R², +Ric², both) against analytic form factors.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-14T09:39:28Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-14T09:39:28Z"}

new_value: {"status":"in_progress"}
old_value: {"id":"TGR-om8","title":"HANDOFF documents wrong formula for EH bilinear: use h^{ab}δ¹G_{ab} not δ²R+cross","description":"The HANDOFFs from sessions 30-31 use the formula δ²(√gR) = δ²R + (1/2)tr(h)δ¹R. While algebraically correct, this decomposition is WRONG for kernel extraction because the cross term (1/2)tr(h)δ¹R has n_L=0,n_R=2 and its spin projection gives (0, 1.5) not the expected (1.25, -0.5). The CORRECT approach for the EH bilinear form is h^{ab}δ¹G_{ab} = h^{ab}(δ¹R_{ab} - (1/2)g_{ab}δ¹R), which gives the FP kernel directly. Verified: spin-2=2.5, spin-0s=-1.0.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-14T09:39:18Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-14T09:39:18Z"}

new_value: {"notes":"Session 32 progress: flat-space crosscheck COMPLETE via extract_kernel_direct (h^{ab}d1G_{ab} matches FP to machine precision). dS crosscheck: gauge sectors partly fixed (spin-0w=0 at all Lambda) but spin-1 has residual proportional to Lambda. Root cause: the bilinear h^{ab}(d1G_{ab}+Lambda*h_{ab}) needs additional background curvature corrections from the contracted Bianchi identity on MSS. The old pipeline (Q=d2R+sqrt_g_correction) may be needed for dS, adapted to use extract_kernel_direct. Key finding: the -Lambda*h_{ab}h^{ab}+(1/2)Lambda*h^2 cosmological correction fixes spin-0w but not spin-1."}

new_value: {"status":"in_progress"}
old_value: {"id":"TGR-68n","title":"spin_project has index collision on dS kernels — free indices leak into scalar result","description":"When projecting kernels extracted from MSS (de Sitter) expressions, spin_project produces terms with free indices (e.g. k^o k^o, g^{oo}) instead of a fully scalar result. Root cause: the projector's internal dummy indices (from theta/omega) collide with coefficient indices after ensure_no_dummy_clash. At Λ=0 (flat limit) this doesn't manifest because Λ-dependent terms vanish. At finite Λ, the collision creates uncontractable free index pairs. Diagnosis: inspecting spin_project(K_EH_dS, :spin2) shows 3 of 7 terms have 2 free Up indices named 'o'. Fix needed: ensure all dummy indices in the projector are unique w.r.t. the full coefficient expression.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-14T17:20:28Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-14T17:20:28Z"}

new_value: {"notes":"The residual g^{oo} issue is handled by _eval_spin_scalar fallback contraction. The REAL blocker for dS crosscheck is that flat Barnes-Rivers projectors give wrong results on dS kernels — the projectors need MSS adaptation (background curvature modifies the eigenvalue spectrum). This is a theoretical extension (dS-adapted spin projectors), not a bug in the existing code."}

Fix needed: ensure all dummy indices in the projector are unique w.r.t. the full coefficient expression.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-14T17:20:28Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-14T17:20:40Z"}

old_value: {"id":"TGR-zkw","title":"Auto-register Killing equation as rewrite rules","description":"Extend define_killing\\!(reg, :ξ; manifold, metric) to automatically register the Killing equation £_ξ g_{ab} = ∇_a ξ_b + ∇_b ξ_a = 0 as a RewriteRule. Also add check_killing(reg, :ξ, expr) that simplifies an expression using Killing rules. Located in src/gr/killing.jl. ~80 lines.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-12T18:52:40Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-12T18:52:40Z"}

old_value: {"id":"TGR-3gx","title":"Implement order-2 curvature syzygies as rewrite rules","description":"Add syzygies to src/gr/invariants.jl: (1) Gauss-Bonnet identity in 4D: E₄ = R² - 4Ric² + Kretschmann is topological → Weyl² = Kretschmann - 2Ric² + R²/3; (2) In 3D: Weyl=0 → Kretschmann = 2Ric² - R²/2; (3) register_invariant_syzygies!(reg; dim) that adds these as RewriteRules; (4) simplify_invariants(expr; reg, dim) that applies syzygies to reduce to independent basis. Depends on dim from manifold registry. ~150 lines.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-12T18:54:48Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-12T18:54:48Z"}

old_value: {"id":"TGR-0mg","title":"Implement Gauss-Codazzi relations as rewrite rules","description":"Add src/gr/gauss_codazzi.jl with: (1) gauss_equation(reg, hyp) returning RewriteRule: projected ambient Riemann → intrinsic Riemann + extrinsic curvature terms: R^(γ)_{ijkl} = P^a_i P^b_j P^c_k P^d_l R_{abcd} + K_{ik}K_{jl} - K_{il}K_{jk}; (2) codazzi_equation(reg, hyp) returning ∇_i K_{jk} - ∇_j K_{ik} = P^a_i P^b_j P^c_k n^d R_{abcd}; (3) Register as rules via register_rule!. These relate ambient and intrinsic curvature on embedded hypersurfaces. ~200 lines.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-12T18:54:19Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-12T18:54:19Z"}

These are used by the TOV solver to close the hydrostatic equilibrium system. ~100 lines.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-12T18:54:04Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-12T18:54:04Z"}

old_value: {"id":"TGR-f0c","title":"Implement axial symmetry metric ansatz generator","description":"Add metric_ansatz(reg, manifold, ::AxialSymmetry; coords=[:t,:r,:θ,:φ]) returning the most general stationary axisymmetric metric with 4 free functions of (r,θ): ds²=-N²dt²+g_rr dr²+g_θθ dθ²+g_φφ(dφ-ωdt)² (Lewis-Papapetrou form). Cross-term g_{tφ}=-g_φφ·ω encodes frame dragging. Return (metric, free_functions=[N,g_rr,g_θθ,g_φφ,ω]). Test: verify Kerr metric is special case with known N,ω,g_rr,g_θθ,g_φφ. ~200 lines.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-12T18:52:39Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-12T18:52:39Z"}

old_value: {"id":"TGR-760","title":"Implement GHY boundary term extraction","description":"Add to src/gr/hypersurface.jl: (1) ghy_boundary_term(reg, hypersurface) returning ∫_∂M √h K where h=induced metric determinant, K=trace of extrinsic curvature; (2) As TensorExpr: 2*K where K = g^{ab}K_{ab}; (3) Add ibp_with_boundary(expr, field; registry) variant of ibp() that returns (bulk_term, boundary_term) instead of discarding boundary. Modify src/algebra/ibp.jl to support this. The GHY term is essential for well-posed variational problems with boundaries. Test: verify δ(S_EH + S_GHY)/δg = 0 gives correct Einstein equations. ~200 lines.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-12T18:54:12Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-12T18:54:12Z"}

old_value: {"id":"TGR-141","title":"Add order-3 cubic curvature invariants to catalog","description":"Extend src/gr/invariants.jl with the 6 independent cubic invariants (already computed in benchmarks/bench_12_6deriv_dS.jl): I₁=R³, I₂=R·Ric², I₃=Ric³ (trace), I₄=R·Riem², I₅=Ric·Riem² (contraction), I₆=Riem³ (cycle). Port the build_I1..build_I6 functions from bench_12 into the invariant catalog. ~200 lines.","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-12T18:54:54Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-12T18:54:54Z"}

108 tests in test_ds_spin_projectors.jl: Vandermonde solver, Λ=0 backward compat, synthetic MSS kernel, EH spin-2/spin-0s matching BC at multiple Λ, 4-deriv Stelle theory, Lichnerowicz mass identification, edge cases.
